sutta: 4 uh but this suta is a little bit more for monks and buddha said just as monks when a young wife is first led home to her husband either by day online she at first feels exceeding great fear and bashfulness in the presence of her mother-in-law her father-in-law her husband and even towards servants and work people but as time goes on owing to living together and intimacy she addresses mother-in-law father-in-law and husband thus away with you what do you know just in like manner monks a certain monk here maybe when he first goes forth whether by day or night from the home to the homeless life feels exceeding great fear and bashfulness in the presence of monks and nuns disciples male and female even of novices who serve in the monastery but as time goes on owing to living together and intimacy he addresses teacher and preceptor thus away with you what do you know where 4 months thus must you train yourselves i will dwell in mind like a young wife newly arrived that is how you must train yourselves in this utah we can see here that the tradition in india is that when when a man and woman marries the wife goes to live with the husband's family and that's the indian tradition so the buddha is saying when a new wife is brought to the home of the husband she's very shy very scared but later after many years she becomes very arrogant uh it's possible so in the same way the buddha said a new monk when he first goes forth he might be very scared of the superiors scan of the teacher acharya and the preceptor upajaya but later he becomes arrogant and he talks very close with them so the buddha said this should be that monk should always behave as though he's a young wife like just the simile of the young wife newly arrived and must be respectful to the superior monks but in a way this suta can also be applied to lay people the simile of the wife newly married and somebody is newly married and after many years should not be arrogant now we come to the next suta uh
